name: Blue Cross Blue Shield
wegovy_coverage: Varies by plan and state licensee. BCBS of Michigan ended coverage of all GLP-1 weight-loss drugs (Wegovy, Zepbound, Saxenda) for fully-insured large-group commercial members effective January 1, 2025; self-funded employer plans may still cover. Coverage genuinely varies across the 33 independent BCBS licensees.
ozempic_coverage: Generally covered for type 2 diabetes diagnosis with prior authorization on most BCBS plans.
mounjaro_coverage: Generally covered for type 2 diabetes; step therapy through metformin commonly required.
zepbound_coverage: Varies by plan and licensee. BCBS of Michigan large-group commercial members lost Zepbound coverage as of Jan 1, 2025. Other licensees vary.

verification.notes: BCBS is 33 independent licensees and there is no single BCBS national clinical policy bulletin for GLP-1s. BCBS of Michigan is cited as a representative large licensee that publicly ended fully-insured large-group commercial coverage of Wegovy/Zepbound/Saxenda effective Jan 1, 2025. Other licensees (BCBS Texas, Highmark, Anthem-licensed BCBS plans, Florida Blue, etc.) all maintain their own policies and BMI/step-therapy criteria. Member count 119.1M = BCBSA aggregate per Mark Farrah Associates 3Q25; note significant overlap with Anthem/Elevance row since Elevance operates BCBS-licensed plans in 14 states. Confidence is LOW because the row is by necessity directional. Generic copay range, typical Ozempic/Mounjaro language, and typical PA requirement retained as directional summaries — not traceable to a single bulletin.

name: UnitedHealthcare
wegovy_coverage: UnitedHealthcare/OptumRx maintains Wegovy (injection and tablet) on the commercial Pharmacy Benefit Programs Drug List with prior authorization. Initial authorization is typically 5 months. Coverage on individual plans depends on the specific PDL the employer has selected; some employer groups exclude weight-loss-only GLP-1s.
ozempic_coverage: Generally covered for type 2 diabetes with prior authorization.
mounjaro_coverage: Generally covered for type 2 diabetes; step therapy through metformin typically required.
zepbound_coverage: Zepbound is included in UnitedHealthcare's commercial weight-loss/appetite-suppression coverage with prior authorization. Initial authorization typically 6 months. Excluded by some employer-funded plans.

verification.notes: Sources are UHC Provider site Pharmacy Benefit Programs Drug List (effective Apr 1, 2026) and the UHC Commercial Weight-Loss PA Notification. Per these documents, Wegovy injection, Wegovy tablet, and Zepbound are all on UHC's commercial weight-loss list with PA. Prior wave-3 note that UHC 'removed Wegovy from many formularies' was directionally inaccurate for the standard commercial PDL — the more accurate framing is that some self-funded employer groups have excluded weight-loss GLP-1s while UHC's standard commercial PDL retains them with PA. Member count 49.8M from UHG 2025 year-end earnings release. Specific BMI threshold and step-therapy criteria are detailed in the PA Notification PDF but not reproduced here verbatim — generic 'BMI 30, or 27 with comorbidity' criteria are FDA labeling defaults, not extracted from a UHC bulletin, so not asserted in this row.

name: Aetna
wegovy_coverage: Aetna's Non-Medicare Prescription Drug Plan covers Wegovy under PA Policy 4774-C with quantity limits. Per the published PA criteria: BMI requirements consistent with FDA labeling, prior authorization required, documentation of a structured weight-management program (diet, exercise, behavioral) for at least 6 months, and demonstrated weight loss of at least 5% on a stable maintenance dose to continue therapy.
ozempic_coverage: Covered for type 2 diabetes diagnosis with standard prior authorization on Aetna commercial plans.
mounjaro_coverage: Covered for type 2 diabetes; some plans require step therapy.
zepbound_coverage: Coverage on Aetna commercial plans varies by employer group. Aetna Medicare plans do not cover Zepbound for weight-loss-only indications (federal Part D statutory exclusion).

verification.notes: Primary sources: Aetna Non-Medicare Pharmacy Clinical Policy Bulletin 'Wegovy_PA_with_Limit_4774-C' (the live Wegovy PA policy on aetna.com) and Aetna Medical CPB 0039 'Weight Reduction Programs and Devices'. Note: the Wave 3 entry referenced CPB 0625 — that bulletin number does not appear in current Aetna search results; the relevant medical CPB for weight reduction is 0039, and the pharmacy PA policy is 4774-C. Specific BMI thresholds and 6-month structured weight-management program requirement are derived from the Aetna pharmacy PA policy summaries surfaced in search results but the exact PA bulletin text was not opened (WebFetch unavailable in this session). Member count 39M directionally consistent with Aetna's reported medical membership but not independently re-verified for this pass.

name: Cigna
wegovy_coverage: Cigna covers Wegovy on many commercial plans with prior authorization. Per the published Cigna GLP-1 weight-loss PA coverage policy: BMI >=30, or BMI >=27 with a weight-related comorbidity (hypertension, dyslipidemia, type 2 diabetes); patient must be at least 18; documented reduced-calorie diet for at least 3 months prior; concurrent lifestyle modification. Initial approval up to 8 months. Reauthorization requires documented >=5% body-weight loss; reauthorization up to 12 months.
ozempic_coverage: Covered for type 2 diabetes with prior authorization on most Cigna plans.
mounjaro_coverage: Covered for type 2 diabetes; step therapy through metformin or another GLP-1 commonly required.
zepbound_coverage: Covered under the same Cigna GLP-1 weight-loss PA policy framework as Wegovy (BMI >=30 or >=27 with comorbidity, 3-month diet documentation, lifestyle changes, 5% weight loss for continuation). Some Express Scripts/Cigna employer footprints apply additional cost caps or plan-level exclusions.

verification.notes: Primary sources: Cigna 'Glucagon-Like Peptide-1 Agonists Prior Authorization' coverage policies cnf_684 (weight-loss GLP-1 PA), ip_0206 (weight-loss GLP-1 drug coverage policy), and cnf_360 (general GLP-1 PA). BMI thresholds, 3-month diet documentation, 8-month initial approval, and 5%-weight-loss reauthorization criterion are summarized from the published policies (search-result extraction; WebFetch not available in this session, so verbatim text was not pulled). Member count 18M is directionally consistent with Cigna Healthcare medical membership but not re-verified for this pass.

name: Humana
wegovy_coverage: Humana Medicare Advantage Part D plans exclude Wegovy when prescribed for weight loss. Humana applies an automated ICD-10 edit at the point of sale: GLP-1 claims with an obesity diagnosis (E66.x) auto-reject; claims with a diabetes diagnosis (E11.x) or sleep apnea (G47.33) may pay. Wegovy may be covered under Part D for the cardiovascular-risk-reduction indication consistent with CMS March 2024 guidance.
ozempic_coverage: Covered for type 2 diabetes diagnosis on most Humana plans.
mounjaro_coverage: Covered for type 2 diabetes; some plans require step therapy.
zepbound_coverage: Excluded by Humana Medicare Advantage when prescribed for weight loss (Part D statutory exclusion). May be coverable for the FDA-approved obstructive sleep apnea indication subject to plan criteria.

verification.notes: No Humana-published clinical policy bulletin URL was located in this pass. The Wegovy/Zepbound weight-loss exclusion on Humana MA-PD is downstream of the Medicare Part D statutory exclusion of weight-loss-only drugs (SSA 1860D-2(e)(2)(A)) and is documented in HHS ASPE's 'Medicare Coverage of Anti-Obesity Medications' brief and CMS's 'Medicare GLP-1 Bridge' page. The ICD-10 auto-edit description is sourced from third-party reporting (findhonestcare.com) and is NOT yet traced to a Humana primary document — treat as directional. Commercial Humana coverage rules are not asserted because no Humana commercial PDL bulletin URL was located. Member count 17M not re-verified.

name: Anthem
wegovy_coverage: Coverage varies by Anthem-licensed plan and state. Elevance Health (Anthem's parent) added Wegovy for the cardiovascular-risk-reduction indication on supported plans. Effective January 1, 2026, Anthem Medi-Cal removed Wegovy, Zepbound, and Saxenda from coverage when prescribed for weight loss; PA requests are still considered for non-weight-loss indications (Wegovy for MASH or established CVD, Zepbound for obstructive sleep apnea).
ozempic_coverage: Covered for type 2 diabetes diagnosis in most Anthem plans.
mounjaro_coverage: Covered for type 2 diabetes; step therapy may apply.
zepbound_coverage: Varies by Anthem plan. Anthem Medi-Cal excludes Zepbound for weight loss as of Jan 1, 2026 but considers PA for OSA. Virginia Anthem Medicaid criteria require BMI >40, or BMI >37 with risk factors (dyslipidemia, hypertension).

verification.notes: Primary sources: Anthem CA Medi-Cal 'GLP-1 Coverage Considerations 11/14/2025' provider notice (documents the Jan 1, 2026 Medi-Cal removal of Wegovy/Zepbound/Saxenda for weight loss), Anthem VA Medicaid Weight-Loss Management PA Form (BMI >40 / >37 with risk factors criteria), and Anthem Medical Drug Clinical Criteria CC-0188. MEMBER-COUNT OVERLAP: Anthem/Elevance is the licensed BCBS operator in 14 states, so the 47M member count overlaps materially with the 119.1M BCBS aggregate row above and these two rows should NOT be summed as independent populations. Elevance reported ~46M medical members in recent earnings; 47M retained as approximate. Commercial Anthem Wegovy/Zepbound criteria vary by employer PDL and are not asserted as a single rule in this row.

name: Kaiser Permanente
wegovy_coverage: Per Kaiser Permanente's published 2026 Southern California Commercial HMO formulary and Northwest drug-specific monographs, Wegovy is restricted on Kaiser commercial formularies. Kaiser FEHB and PSHB plans place Zepbound on Tier 3/4 (specialty) with 50% coinsurance after PA and weight-management program participation. Kaiser added Wegovy for the cardiovascular-risk-reduction indication on supported plans.
ozempic_coverage: Covered for type 2 diabetes diagnosis through Kaiser pharmacy.
mounjaro_coverage: Covered for type 2 diabetes; Kaiser providers typically prefer semaglutide first line.
zepbound_coverage: Limited. Available through Kaiser obesity-medicine programs in some regions under strict criteria (failed semaglutide trial, BMI thresholds). Kaiser FEHB/PSHB places Zepbound on Tier 3/4 with PA and weight-management program participation.

verification.notes: Primary sources: Kaiser Permanente Southern California Commercial HMO formulary (March 2026 update), Kaiser Northwest Wegovy and Zepbound drug monographs, Kaiser Mid-Atlantic Weight Management PA form. Kaiser is an integrated delivery system so its 'formulary' decisions are made regionally and the same drug can be on different tiers across KP regions. The 'limited' covers_glp1 designation reflects that Kaiser tightened weight-loss GLP-1 access on its core commercial formularies in 2025-2026 while continuing access through obesity-medicine programs and on FEHB/PSHB tiers. Member count 12.7M consistent with KP's reported membership.

name: Medicare
wegovy_coverage: Medicare Part D excludes drugs prescribed for weight loss under the statutory exclusion at SSA 1860D-2(e)(2)(A). Per CMS HPMS memo issued March 20, 2024, Wegovy may be covered under Part D when prescribed for the FDA-approved cardiovascular-risk-reduction indication (reducing risk of MACE in adults with established CVD who are obese or overweight, supported by the SELECT trial). Plan-level coverage and PA criteria vary.
ozempic_coverage: Covered under Medicare Part D for type 2 diabetes diagnosis.
mounjaro_coverage: Covered under Medicare Part D for type 2 diabetes diagnosis.
zepbound_coverage: Not covered under Medicare Part D for weight loss (statutory exclusion). Zepbound has a separate FDA approval for moderate-to-severe obstructive sleep apnea in adults with obesity; Part D coverage for the OSA indication is plan-dependent.

verification.notes: Primary sources: CMS 'Medicare GLP-1 Bridge' coverage page, HHS ASPE 'Medicare Coverage of Anti-Obesity Medications' issue brief, and KFF analysis of the March 2024 CMS HPMS memo. The CMS HPMS memo titled 'Part D Coverage of Anti-Obesity Medications with Medically Accepted Indications' was distributed during the week of March 18-22, 2024 (commonly cited as March 20, 2024). The Wave 3 row's framing of the SSA exclusion plus the CV carve-out is correct and is now anchored to primary CMS/HHS sources. Member count 67M consistent with total Medicare beneficiaries.

name: Medicaid
wegovy_coverage: Coverage varies dramatically by state. Per KFF's January 2026 update, 13 state Medicaid programs cover GLP-1s for obesity treatment under fee-for-service (down from 16 in 2025). California Medi-Cal ENDED coverage of Wegovy/Zepbound/Saxenda for weight loss effective January 1, 2026. Pennsylvania Medicaid ended weight-loss GLP-1 coverage for adults 21+ effective January 1, 2026. North Carolina Medicaid REINSTATED weight-management GLP-1 coverage on December 12, 2025. The CMS BALANCE Model announced December 23, 2025 offers participating states $245/month GLP-1 pricing.
ozempic_coverage: Covered in nearly all state Medicaid programs for type 2 diabetes diagnosis.
mounjaro_coverage: Covered in most state Medicaid programs for type 2 diabetes.
zepbound_coverage: Coverage varies by state. A small number of states cover Zepbound for obesity with strict criteria. California excludes as of Jan 1, 2026.

verification.notes: CRITICAL CORRECTION FROM WAVE 3: prior row claimed 'CA, MN, PA, RI cover Wegovy for obesity.' Per KFF's January 2026 update, that list is OUT OF DATE — California ENDED coverage Jan 1, 2026 and Pennsylvania ENDED coverage for adults 21+ on Jan 1, 2026. The current count is 13 FFS Medicaid programs covering GLP-1s for obesity (down from 16 in 2025), and the precise list is in flux. Specific state names are not enumerated in this row to avoid asserting an out-of-date list; consult the live KFF tracker. Member count 90M directionally reasonable for total Medicaid + CHIP enrollment but not re-verified for this pass.

name: TRICARE (Military)
wegovy_coverage: Covered by TRICARE Prime and TRICARE Select with prior authorization through Express Scripts (TPHARM contractor for DoD). Effective August 31, 2025, weight-loss medications including Wegovy and Zepbound are no longer covered for non-Prime / non-Select beneficiaries. Initial PA approval is for 12 months; annual renewal requires the original TRICARE PA. Wegovy may be covered for patients age 12+ meeting criteria.
ozempic_coverage: Covered for type 2 diabetes diagnosis under the TRICARE pharmacy benefit.
mounjaro_coverage: Covered for type 2 diabetes; step therapy may apply.
zepbound_coverage: Covered for TRICARE Prime/Select beneficiaries age 18+ with prior authorization. Zepbound is NOT approved for patients under 18 under TRICARE PA criteria. Not covered for non-Prime/non-Select beneficiaries as of Aug 31, 2025.

verification.notes: Primary sources: TRICARE Express Scripts FAQ on GLP-1/weight-loss coverage, tricare.mil Wegovy pharmacy program FAQ, health.mil TRICARE Formulary page, and tricare.mil PA/medical-necessity page. The Aug 31, 2025 termination of weight-loss-medication coverage for non-Prime / non-Select beneficiaries is documented in the Express Scripts TRICARE FAQ. Pediatric criteria (under 12 not approved; 12-18 with additional criteria; Zepbound not approved under 18) are summarized from the Express Scripts PA form. Member count 9.6M consistent with DHA reporting of total TRICARE beneficiaries.
